title: Coelogyne graciliscapa
taxonomy: Coelogyne graciliscapa is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Coelogyne. This genus belongs to the subtribe Coelogyninae, which is part of the family Orchidaceae. Orchidaceae is the botanical name for the orchid family, one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. The Coelogyne genus is native to tropical Asia, and Coelogyne graciliscapa is a species found in this region. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the orchid family.
uses: Coelogyne graciliscapa has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. However, research on the plant's specific uses is limited. In some Asian countries, it is used to treat respiratory issues and fever. The plant's extracts have also shown potential antimicrobial properties, which could aid in the treatment of certain infections. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Coelogyne graciliscapa.
